name: Salesforce, Inc.

profile.description: Salesforce, Inc. provides customer relationship management technology services that connect companies and customers together in the United States, Europe, and the Asia Pacific. The company offers Agentforce, which enables customers to build, deploy, and manage enterprise-grade, autonomous AI agents at scale, enabling humans and agents to work together; Agentforce Sales, an integrated platform that brings together the power of humans with AI agents to help sales teams for selling, managing, and automating entire sales processes; Agentforce Service, which enables companies in every industry to bring all of their customer, employee, IT, and field service needs onto one integrated AI-powered platform; Data 360, a data engine that gives AI agents their context and serves as the foundation for how customers unify service offerings, making their data actionable for both humans and agents; Informatica, an AI-powered data management platform that enables customers to discover, integrate, govern, and deliver trusted data at scale across hybrid and multi-cloud environments; and Slack, a conversational interface for the agentic enterprise where people and agents work together, connecting knowledge, actions, and data in real time. It also provides marketing platforms; commerce services, which empower shopping experiences across various customer touchpoints; integration and analytics solutions; Salesforce Starter, a suite for small and medium-sized businesses that brings sales, service, marketing, and commerce together; and a field service solution that enables companies to connect service agents, dispatchers, and mobile employees through one centralized platform to schedule and dispatch work, as well as track and manage jobs. It serves financial services, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, automotive, and government sectors. Salesforce, Inc. was incorporated in 1999 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Salesforce has executed a transformative pivot from CRM software to an AI-first enterprise platform with Agentforce, achieving $1B+ in Agentforce ARR and $3.4B in combined AI and data ARR [1]. The platform consumed nearly 20 trillion tokens and converted them into 2.4 billion agentic work units, demonstrating AI at massive production scale. This is a company-defining transformation comparable to the original SaaS pivot. AI Deployment (2/2): Agentforce is shipping to 29,000+ customers with ARR growing 169% YoY to $800M, processing 2.4 billion agentic work units. This is measurable, quantified AI revenue at scale [1]. Market Share (1/2): Dominant CRM platform globally, though market share growth is slowing with FY2026 revenue up only 10% versus historical 20%+ growth. Pipeline (1/2): FY2027 guidance implies continued growth, with management targeting $63B revenue by FY2030 [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Massive R&D investment in AI, Einstein, and Agentforce platform capabilities.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Marc Benioff's 'the business of business is improving the state of the world' is specific, genuine, and validated by the 1-1-1 philanthropy model (1% equity, 1% product, 1% employee time) that has operated since founding and been adopted by hundreds of other companies [1]. However, recent layoffs and efficiency focus under pressure from activist investors have tested this commitment. Community Impact (2/2): The 1-1-1 model has generated billions in philanthropic impact, with Salesforce.org providing free/discounted CRM to nonprofits. The model is genuinely distinctive and industry-leading. Employee Retention (0/2): Significant layoffs in 2023-2024 (10,000+) and activist investor pressure have disrupted the employee value proposition. Insider Buying (1/2): Benioff maintains significant equity and has made public stock purchases, though insider selling also occurs regularly.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. FY2026 revenue of $41.5B grew 10% YoY with total RPO of $72B (+14%), providing strong revenue visibility [1]. Net income of $7.46B for FY2026 represents meaningful profitability improvement. The company has pivoted toward margin expansion and capital returns, declaring dividends and executing share buybacks. Forward P/E and PEG ratio reflect a mature enterprise software franchise.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Salesforce's core CRM platform faces moderate AI disruption risk — AI-native CRM tools from competitors could theoretically replace traditional Salesforce workflows [1]. However, the massive installed base, complex enterprise integrations, and switching costs create significant defensive moats. The bigger risk is that AI agents reduce the need for human CRM interaction, compressing the TAM for CRM software. AI Opportunity (1/1): Agentforce ARR of $1B+ and combined AI/Data ARR of $3.4B demonstrate that Salesforce is actively monetizing AI at significant scale [1].

infinite_game.summary: Salesforce under Benioff has been a genuine infinite game player — the 1-1-1 model, stakeholder capitalism advocacy, and long-term platform investments reflect purpose-driven leadership. However, the 2023-2024 pivot to efficiency under activist investor pressure revealed that finite game pressures can override the cause when stock performance demands it.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Benioff's vision that technology should serve all stakeholders, not just shareholders, has been genuinely lived through the 1-1-1 model and Salesforce.org [1]. However, the capitulation to activist investors (layoffs, margin focus, reduced philanthropic rhetoric) shows the cause hasn't been fully validated through crisis.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Pre-2023 Salesforce was known for exceptional employee culture ('Ohana'). The 10,000+ layoffs and activist-driven efficiency pivot damaged this trust significantly. The current culture is rebuilding but has been fundamentally altered.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Salesforce acknowledges Microsoft (Dynamics + Copilot) as a worthy rival whose AI capabilities push Salesforce to innovate faster. Competitive language focuses on platform superiority rather than competitor destruction.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Salesforce's pivot from CRM to AI-first platform (Agentforce, Data Cloud) represents meaningful strategic evolution, though not the existential-level reinvention of abandoning a core business.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Benioff has taken principled public stances (Indiana religious freedom law, homelessness tax in San Francisco) that cost real political capital. However, the 2023-2024 layoffs and efficiency pivot under activist pressure represent choosing near-term stock price over long-term purpose.

investment_thesis: Salesforce delivered FY2026 revenue of $41.5B (+10%) with Agentforce ARR surging to $1B+ and combined AI/Data ARR reaching $3.4B, demonstrating successful transformation from CRM to AI enterprise platform [1]. RPO of $72B (+14%) provides strong revenue visibility. Net income of $7.46B reflects the post-restructuring profitability improvement. The company consumed 20 trillion tokens generating 2.4 billion agentic work units, proving AI deployment at production scale. Management targets $63B revenue by FY2030.
thesis_continued: Revenue growth of 10% represents a significant deceleration from Salesforce's historical 20%+ growth, raising questions about whether AI is cannibalizing rather than expanding the core CRM market [1]. The massive AI infrastructure investments required to support Agentforce may compress margins if token costs don't decline. Microsoft Copilot integration into Dynamics and Office represents an existential competitive threat from a company with deeper AI capabilities and broader enterprise distribution. The post-activist Salesforce has sacrificed its culture and philanthropic identity for margin optimization, which may impact long-term talent retention and innovation.
key_catalysts: Agentforce ARR accelerating from $1B+ toward multi-billion dollar scale as enterprise AI adoption reaches critical mass, with 29,000 deals providing strong pipeline visibility
FY2027 revenue reacceleration in H2 as AI products mature and customer deployment expands, building toward the $63B FY2030 target
Margin expansion from AI-driven operational efficiency and pricing power as Salesforce establishes itself as the dominant enterprise AI platform
key_risks: Microsoft Copilot + Dynamics integration threatening Salesforce's core CRM market share as enterprises consolidate AI-powered productivity tools under a single Microsoft umbrella
Revenue growth deceleration from 10% to sub-10% if AI cannibalization reduces traditional CRM seat demand faster than Agentforce revenue scales
Agentforce token economics may not be sustainable at scale — 20 trillion tokens consumed implies massive compute costs that could compress AI product margins
sources: Salesforce FY2026 Full-Year and Q4 Earnings Release, March 2026
Yahoo Finance — CRM Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
